title: Astracantha cuspistipulata
taxonomy: Astracantha cuspistipulata is a species of plant in the legume family, Fabaceae. It is a type of shrub native to the Mediterranean region. This plant is part of the subgenus Astracantha, which is a group of shrubs within the genus Astragalus. Astracantha cuspistipulata is further classified under the kingdom Plantae and phylum Magnoliophyta. Its classification also includes the class Magnoliopsida and order Fabales.
uses: Astracantha cuspistipulata, a species of spiny shrub, has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various ailments. The plant's roots and stems are believed to possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may contribute to its medicinal uses. In traditional medicine, Astracantha cuspistipulata is used to treat conditions such as fever, skin irritations, and respiratory issues. However, further research is needed to confirm the effectiveness and safety of using this plant for medicinal purposes. More studies are required to fully explore the potential benefits and risks of using Astracantha cuspistipulata for human health.

intro: Astracantha cuspistipulata, also known as the "Devil's Claw", is a perennial plant species native to the Middle East and North Africa, belonging to the pea family (Fabaceae). The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, offering potential health benefits such as anti-inflammatory and analgesic effects, which can aid in relieving pain and reducing inflammation. The bioactive constituents of Astracantha cuspistipulata include harpagoside, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its therapeutic properties. The most common herbal preparations made with this plant include capsules, tablets, and teas, often used to treat arthritis, fibromyalgia, and other musculoskeletal conditions.
